sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current arrangement matches top bar order: Red=[O, p, R], Green=[b, N, Y, M]
# But SPACE doesn't complete. Let me try different approaches.

# First, let me check what SPACE is doing by looking at what changes
# Check the progress bar area
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Look for gray objects near the bottom (progress bar)
print("Objects near progress bar (row 50-60):")
for n in nodes:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    min_r = min(rows)
    if 50 <= min_r <= 60:
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        min_c = min(cols)
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, at ({min_r},{min_c})")

# Try clicking on the green connector between frames
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 30, 'col': 31}])
print(f"\nClick connector: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={result.get('board_changed')}")

# Try clicking on the white corner decoration
result2 = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 17}])
print(f"Click white corner: done={result2.get('done')}, level_completed={result2.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={result2.get('board_changed')}")

# Try clicking on the frame border (red frame top)
result3 = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 30}])
print(f"Click red frame border: done={result3.get('done')}, level_completed={result3.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={result3.get('board_changed')}")

# Check if level completed
if result.get('level_completed') or result2.get('level_completed') or result3.get('level_completed'):
    print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
else:
    # Try selecting a slot and pressing SPACE
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 22}])  # Select red[0]
    result4 = action(['SPACE'])
    print(f"Select slot + SPACE: done={result4.get('done')}, level_completed={result4.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={result4.get('board_changed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
